title: Centrosolenia crenata
taxonomy: Centrosolenia crenata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions of the world, specifically in Southeast Asia. Within the Asteraceae family, Centrosolenia crenata is placed in the subfamily Vernonioideae. The classification further categorizes it as a member of the tribe Vernonieae. This placement is based on its physical characteristics and botanical features.
uses: Centrosolenia crenata has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues. Its extracts have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential therapeutic applications.
